data.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (4/N) + adopted critique refinements.

Q8a — execution_mode binding: FIXED ON THE PROMPT (not step-level). A prompt intrinsically IS single_turn or agentic; cleaner contract identity + palette can hard-group by it. A prompt usable both ways must be duplicated (accepted tradeoff). single_turn = ZERO tools (pure transform on with: inputs); agentic = current tool-using pydantic_ai path.

Q8b — v1 SCOPE: PROMPT MODEL + CONTRACTS FIRST; MCP + DEEP TYPE-CHECK = v1.1.
  v1 = decomposed prompt model + contract-home + DERIVED-INDEX LOCKFILE (generated, committed) + scripted-op contracts (lowest-risk, built FIRST to anchor the contract vocabulary) + NAME-EXISTENCE expression validation + read-only inspector + prompt library dropdown + editable prompt write-back.
  v1.1 (separable, env-gated) = the mcp_call track (allowlist/secrets/cached-manifest) + DEEP JSON-Schema type-compatibility checking + properties-panel structured fields.
  Rationale: cutting MCP loses no coherence in the authoring story and removes the largest security surface + the type-system balloon-risk from the critical path.

ADOPTED REFINEMENTS from adversarial design review (fold into spec):
1. Validator is THREE-STATE (OK / UNKNOWN-opaque / ERROR) with an 'unchecked' badge; distinct ${{ inputs.* }} namespace for engine-injected vars (ticket_id/ticket_context/repo_path) vs steps.*.outputs.
2. Derived index = a GENERATED, COMMITTED lockfile enforcing global invariants (single default reviewer; no dimension collisions; preserve langfuse_prompt/fallback_file/applies_to) — validated at build/validate time, NOT a pure runtime scan.
3. Reviewer membership EXPLICIT (category: review / reviewer: true), NEVER inferred from output-schema shape; schema-match is a validation only (keeps transform prompts out of the security-sensitive close gate).
4. MCP server trust: projects may ADD servers but CANNOT shadow/redefine a built-in server name (invert project-name-wins for the server tier); allowlist pins endpoint IDENTITY not just name; cached manifest advisory + reconciled vs trusted allowlist at run time; no auto-trust of freshly-cloned .rebar/.
5. output_contract changes are BREAKING (override/variant changing output shape needs a new name/version); validate flags override-vs-builtin contract drift; write-back ALWAYS shows the resolved target path before Save.
6. Prompt front-matter is FORMAT-VERSIONED; older binaries detect-and-refuse/strip unknown front-matter (don't render YAML into the prompt body — mixed-fleet safety, like TAG_DELTA). NOTE: shipped reviewer .md files have NO front-matter today (parser exists, files don't use it) => adding it is a migration of every built-in prompt (own workstream).
7. Variant contracts INHERIT unless overridden (override = breaking); validate the RESOLVED contract per variant.
8. 'any'-typed (opaque MCP) output is NOT silently mappable into a typed input_contract — requires an author-acknowledged cast.
